panels.description: Medium shot: The artilleryman sits in a dimly lit, ruined basement, leaning forward intensely. He gestures with a jagged piece of metal. Shadows cling to the walls, hinting at a larger, oppressive structure above.

bubbles.text: Nice roomy cages. They'll be caught cheerful, and they'll wonder what people did before Martians took care of them.

panels.description: Close-up: The artilleryman's eyes widen, filled with a feverish, fanatic light. Dust motes dance in a sliver of light illuminating his grime-streaked face.

bubbles.text: Fat and stupid, full of hymns. Or worse—the ones who try to find 'eroticism' in the cage. I've seen it.

panels.description: Medium close-up: The narrator sits opposite, face partially obscured by darkness, looking down with a sense of grim resignation as he listens.

bubbles.text: (Narration) I sat contemplating these things. The logic was as terrifying as the Martians themselves.

panels.description: Wide angle: The artilleryman stands up abruptly, pointing a dirty finger at the floor, his silhouette sharp against the decaying cellar walls.

bubbles.text: We have to build a new life! The tame ones turn to rubbish. We must keep wild, or turn into rats.

panels.description: Low angle: A vision of the London sewers, vast, dark, and interconnected, depicted as a sprawling concrete labyrinth. The artilleryman points toward the floor as if the city above is irrelevant.

bubbles.text: Underground. The drains, the tunnels, the vaults. When London empties, they'll be sweet and clean.
